a: Fleet-Ops > Operations > Orders > + New Order. Pick an Order Config, set pickup/dropoff (+customer/schedule), Save. Open the order > Assign Driver (+vehicle) > Dispatch. The driver gets a Navigator push notification.
Set the schedule date/time when creating the order, or use Operations > Scheduler to place orders on the calendar. Scheduled orders dispatch at their slot when auto-dispatch is enabled.
Operations > Orders > Import. Download the template, fill it, upload, map columns, review the preview, then import — orders are created in bulk.
Open the order for the live map, status timeline, and activity log. Customers use the public tracking link tied to the order's tracking number.
POD is set per Order Config (signature, photo, or scan). The driver captures it in Navigator at dropoff; artifacts attach to the order record.
Operations > Orders > Board view. Drag orders between status columns; columns follow the order config's activity flow.
Fleet-Ops > Resources > Drivers > + New Driver. Enter personal details and license, optionally assign a vehicle and fleet. The driver signs into the Navigator app with their credentials.
Resources > Vehicles > + New Vehicle. Enter VIN, plate, make/model/year; assign to a fleet. Telematics devices attach under the vehicle's Devices tab.
Resources > Places > + New Place. Enter the address (it geocodes automatically). Places serve as pickups, dropoffs, and stations.
Resources > Fleets > + New Fleet, then add vehicles and drivers. Fleets group assets for dispatch scoping and reporting.
Resources > Contacts > + New Contact with type customer. Attach customers to orders for tracking links and invoicing.
Resources > Issues > + New Issue (drivers can also report from Navigator). Set vehicle, category, and priority; triage issues into work orders.
Resources > Fuel Reports > + New Report: vehicle, volume, cost, odometer.
Maintenance > Work Orders > + New. Set subject, priority, the vehicle (VIN-keyed), and assignee. Lifecycle: submitted > scheduled > in progress > repaired > approved > closed.
Only the QC Reviewer role can close a work order. Technicians mark work repaired; QC reviews the evidence (photos/checklist) and approves/closes, or rejects it back with reasons.
Maintenance > Schedules > + New: pick the vehicle, interval method (distance, time, or engine-hours), and threshold. The platform evaluates daily and auto-creates work orders when due.
Maintenance > Parts > + New: SKU, quantity on hand, unit cost. Items at or below their reorder point are flagged for replenishment.
IAM > Users > Invite, then assign a role. Custom roles with per-permission policies live under IAM > Roles. The QC Reviewer role gates work-order closure.
Under Service Areas, draw zones directly on the map. Zones define service boundaries and drive geofence alerts.
Admin > Branding: upload icon and logo; changes apply platform-wide immediately, including the sign-in screen.
Click the brain icon in the header to open CBRE Fleet AI in a docked panel. Ask about live fleet data, product how-tos, or analytics — answers come from the live database and official docs.

Open the console URL, pass the network gate with your email + one-time PIN (invited emails only, 24h sessions), then sign in with your console account. No account? Ask your program administrator for an invite.
